rationale: Acceptance greps (plan.md:693-697) all PASS on both copies; go build + go test -count=1 ./plugins/... green; diff of both ms-panel.js copies identical. Adding a 5th ALLOWED_CLI entry correctly fails TestMsPanelWorkflow_AllowedCLIExactSet (confirmed empirically in a scratch git-worktree). But two concrete gaps found by direct exploitation, both reproduced with runnable evidence (node/go), not just reading: (1) slug and spec are validated only by validatePathElement (ms-panel.js:204-205) -- never validateShellSafe -- yet both are interpolated raw into the SAME shell command-line string buildCommand assembles for CMD_PANEL_CREATE/VERIFY/TALLY (line 78, 95). A slug of 'foo;touch$(echo pwned)`whoami`|cat&' passes validatePathElement cleanly (verified live) and would reach the agent-executed command line -- shell injection via slug/spec, converging independently with R7's finding. target and bead_id get this same guard (validateShellSafe) but slug/spec do not, despite identical exposure. (2) TestMsPanelWorkflow_AllowedCLIExactSet's 'positive enumeration' does NOT hold by construction as its docstring claims: its scanJSLiterals (workflow_test.go:50-93) is a naive char-by-char quote scanner that does not understand JS regex literals. SHELL_METACHAR_RE = /[`;|&\n]|\$\(/ (ms-panel.js:127) contains a literal backtick inside the regex; the scanner mistakes it for a template-literal start and desyncs for the REST of the file (verified: literal offsets go incoherent from byte ~5836 onward). Empirically, I injected a real (non-comment) 'mindspec panel create' literal into runClaudeSlot's prompt string (ms-panel.js:282) and the exact-set test still PASSED -- the guard rail the plan cites as 'strengthens AC4 beyond a present/absent grep' is silently blind past that point. A one-line fix (escaping the backtick as \x60 in SHELL_METACHAR_RE) eliminates the desync and the same injected literal is then correctly caught -- verified both ways. Neither issue is exploited by the current file's own commands (ALLOWED_CLI genuinely has exactly 4 today), but (1) is a live injection path and (2) hollows out the exact test invoked to prove (1) can never regress.
concrete_changes_required: ms-panel.js validateArgs: call validateShellSafe('slug', a.slug) and validateShellSafe('spec', a.spec) in addition to validatePathElement (mirroring bead_id/target) -- slug and spec are interpolated into the same buildCommand shell strings as target/bead_id and currently admit ';', '|', '&', backtick, and '$('.

ms-panel.js:127 SHELL_METACHAR_RE: rewrite the backtick in the character class as an escape that is not a literal ` byte (e.g. /[\x60;|&\n]|\$\(/) so workflow_test.go's scanJSLiterals does not desync on it -- verified this restores positive-enumeration coverage for the whole file (confirmed the previously-undetected injected literal is caught after this change).
findings: CONFIRMED (node, live payload): validatePathElement('slug', 'foo;touch$(echo pwned)`whoami`|cat&') does not throw -- slug/spec lack the shell-metacharacter guard that target/bead_id have, despite identical downstream use in buildCommand's shell command strings (ms-panel.js:78,95,204-205 vs 138-144,153-173).

CONFIRMED (go test in /tmp scratch git-worktree, cleaned up after): injecting a real 'mindspec panel create' string literal into ms-panel.js:282's prompt text (not a comment, not the array) does NOT trip TestMsPanelWorkflow_AllowedCLIExactSet's positive-enumeration, because scanJSLiterals desyncs on the raw backtick inside SHELL_METACHAR_RE (ms-panel.js:127) and never recovers proper string-boundary tracking for the rest of the file (literal offsets become incoherent from byte ~5836 onward, verified via a debug test calling scanJSLiterals directly).

CONFIRMED FIX: escaping that backtick as \x60 (functionally identical regex, verified via node .test() on backtick/semicolon/clean inputs) eliminates the desync; the same injected literal is then correctly flagged by the test.

CONFIRMED (positive control): adding a genuine 5th ALLOWED_CLI array entry ('mindspec panel drop') correctly fails the exact-set count check -- the array-literal exact-set assertion itself is sound, only the whole-file positive-enumeration pass is compromised by the scanner bug.

CONFIRMED (documented, non-issue): a stray 'mindspec panel create' string placed only inside a // comment is correctly NOT flagged by scanJSLiterals (comments are deliberately skipped per its own docstring, ms-panel.js/workflow_test.go:41-49) and separately cannot execute anything -- this is intentional scope, not a gap.

All plan.md Bead-2 acceptance greps (lines 693-697) pass on both tracked copies; go build ./... and go test -count=1 ./plugins/... are green; diff -q of both ms-panel.js copies shows byte-identity; node --check (wrapped as the workflow runtime executes it, since top-level args/await/return are workflow-harness constructs, not raw Node module syntax) is clean.

Every value-interpolation site in ms-panel.js (buildCommand's CMD_PANEL_CREATE/VERIFY/TALLY branches, lines 76-96; the two callers at 438, 491, 505) is downstream of validateArgs (line 428, called before Step 2) except for the slug/spec shell-metachar gap above; panelDir (used for verdict/log write paths at 280,305-306,408) is trusted output of the already-validated `mindspec panel create` invocation, not independent user input, matching the design intent in invariant 5.
